Information about the Molecular Sciences Institute

History and Overview

Dr. Sydney Brenner formed The Molecular Sciences Institute in June of 1996. Dr. Brenner, widely known for his clarity of vision of near term future biology, understood that a key challenge for biological sciences in the first decade of this new millennium would be to distill knowledge and understanding from the surplus of data produced by new observation- and computation-based technologies.

In 1997 Dr. Roger Brent, who had developed a similar vision, agreed to join the Institute.

In early 1998, the Institute commenced operations in its present form in Berkeley, California: as an independent nonprofit research institute aiming to weave physics, engineering, computer science, and mathematics together with biology, genetics and chemistry to enable science that would result in a deeper understanding of the behaviors of complex biological systems. An additional and explicit social goal of the Institute is to encourage young researchers to independently explore new ideas and to nurture a free and innovative scientific environment beyond the constraints imposed by conventional academic, corporate, and government organizations.

Today, the Institute, directed by Drs. Brenner and Brent, consists of a core group of Ph.D. research fellows drawn from biology, physics, computer science, engineering, and math. Operating funds are provided through government grants and by corporate gifts.

Contacts

(510) 647-0694 or brenner@molsci.org

Location

The Molecular Sciences Institute
2168 Shattuck Avenue
Berkeley, CA 94704 USA

The Molecular Sciences Institute is in downtown Berkeley, California near the Berkeley BART stop. If you are driving to the Institute, there are parking garages on Allston Way and Center Street.

Employment

We encourage applications from biologists, physicists, computational scientists, programmers, mathematicians, engineers, chemists, et cetera. Applicants should have an outstanding record of scientific achievement and an explicit desire and ability to further the mission of the Institute. To apply, please send a letter and CV with a list of references to:

Human Resources-Scientific Recruiting
The Molecular Sciences Institute
2168 Shattuck Avenue
Berkeley, CA 94704 USA

Staff and administrative positions are listed here as well (no current openings).
